name: University Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Washtenaw County, MI with personal banking, business lending, mortgages, and wealth management through University Bank and affiliated subsidiaries.
description_long:
University Bank is a community-focused financial institution based in Ann Arbor, Michigan, serving the Washtenaw County area including Ann Arbor and Ypsilanti. The bank operates as a full-service financial institution with multiple subsidiaries and affiliated companies that extend its service offerings across residential and commercial lending, insurance, and specialized mortgage services. The bank has established itself as a local employer and community leader, winning USA Today's Top Work Places award in 2025 and earning multiple community recognitions including Best of Washtenaw County awards and Ann Arbor Family Favorite designations.

University Bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including personal checking and savings accounts, business lending with SBA loan options, home equity loans, bridge loans, residential mortgages through University Lending Group, and wealth management services. The bank operates affiliated entities including Ann Arbor Insurance Centre (30+ years in insurance), University Insurance Finance for faith-based lending, University Bank Warehouse for nationwide mortgage warehouse lending, and Midwest Loan Services for loan servicing. Additionally, the bank has launched a Neighborly Housing Grant program providing up to $10,000 toward down payments for qualified Washtenaw County residents.

What distinguishes University Bank is its deeply rooted local presence and community-focused mission. The bank recently opened a new Ypsilanti Banking Center, operates multiple physical locations, and demonstrates commitment to financial literacy through workshops and community programs. Their integrated subsidiary model allows customers to access insurance, wealth management, and specialized lending through affiliated companies. The Neighborly Housing Grant is a distinctive offering that directly supports homeownership accessibility in their service area.

As a regional community bank, University Bank's primary limitation is geographic—services are primarily available in Washtenaw County, Michigan, making it unsuitable for customers outside this region. The bank does not appear to specialize in credit repair, emergency lending, or products for credit-challenged consumers. While the website emphasizes their community credentials and award recognition, specific product details like interest rates, fees, and APRs are not disclosed on the public website, requiring direct contact for pricing information.
services: Neighborly Housing Grant program (up to $10,000 down payment assistance)
Insurance products through Ann Arbor Insurance Centre
Loan servicing through Midwest Loan Services
pros: Offers Neighborly Housing Grant providing up to $10,000 toward down payments for qualified local residents
Multiple physical banking centers in Ann Arbor and newly opened Ypsilanti location with extended hours
USA Today Top Work Places 2025 award winner, indicating strong employee satisfaction and operational quality
Comprehensive subsidiary ecosystem including insurance (Ann Arbor Insurance Centre), wealth management, and mortgage servicing under one corporate family
SBA small business lending program with dedicated business lending team
24/7 online banking and mobile app access with real-time notifications and fund transfer capabilities
Faith-based financing division (University Insurance Finance) serving niche lending needs
cons: Geographic limitation: Services primarily available only in Washtenaw County, Michigan, excluding most U.S. consumers
No emergency lending or payday alternative products advertised for urgent short-term cash needs
No pricing, interest rates, or fee information disclosed on public website; requires direct contact for quotes
No credit repair, credit building, or credit monitoring products offered
Website lacks specific product details, eligibility requirements, and processing timelines for major services like mortgages and business loans
best_for: Washtenaw County, Michigan residents seeking full-service banking with community accountability and local decision-making
First-time homebuyers in the Ann Arbor/Ypsilanti area eligible for the Neighborly Housing Grant down payment assistance
Small business owners in Southeast Michigan needing SBA lending and local commercial banking relationships
Faith-based organizations and individuals seeking religiously-aligned financing solutions through University Insurance Finance
diagnosis: University Bank is best for residents and small business owners within Washtenaw County, Michigan seeking a community bank with local accountability, integrated services, and special programs like down payment assistance. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—this institution does not serve customers outside Michigan's Washtenaw County region, making it irrelevant for most U.S. consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
